channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PPRA Reports 79% Procurement Compliance but Gaps Still Affect Public Projects
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania's public procurement compliance has improved, reaching 79 percent in the 2024/25 financial year, up from 76 percent in the previous year and 63.5 percent in 2021/22, according...

item.title: Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet enjoy tropical holiday with beach and yacht moments
item.description: Kylie Jenner, the 28-year-old makeup mogul, shared a series of Instagram photos from her spring break holiday in a tropical location she described as 'heaven'. Accompanying her was her boyfriend, Timo...

item.title: No one won the major PCSO lotto jackpots on March 30
item.description: On Monday, March 30, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that there were no winners for the major lottery draws. For the Megalotto 6/45, the winning combination of 03-27-2...

item.title: Grace and Family Move to Mabati House to Manage Finances and Build New Life
item.description: A Nairobi-based woman named Grace recently shared how she and her family had to move from a fancy rented apartment to an unfinished mabati house to cope with financial difficulties. Life had become co...

item.title: Fire at Imtiaz Superstore in Zamzama, Karachi Brought Under Control After Rescue Efforts
item.description: KARACHI: Early Monday morning, a major fire broke out at Imtiaz Superstore in the upscale Zamzama area of Clifton. The blaze started at an ATM inside the store, most probably caused by a short circuit...

item.title: Gachagua hints at Kalonzo as possible United Opposition presidential flagbearer for 2027
item.description: During a five-day tour of the Ukambani region, Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP) leader Rigathi Gachagua signalled strong support for Wiper Party leader Kalonzo Musyoka as a potential presidentia...

item.title: Zanzibar boosts healthcare efficiency through digital records
item.description: Zanzibar President Dr Husein Ali Mwinyi has highlighted the progress in the health sector after moving from paper-based systems to digital platforms. At the Zanzibar Digital Health Conference, he said...

item.title: Edmonton Space Blogger Covers Historic Artemis II Mission with Canadian Astronaut Aboard
item.description: As NASA prepares for the historic Artemis II mission — the first crewed journey around the Moon in more than 50 years — the excitement is palpable at the Kennedy Space Center in Florida. Among the 800...

item.title: Lawyer for Accused in B.C. Officer Killing Seeks Mental Fitness Ruling Ahead of Trial
item.description: A British Columbia man accused of killing a police officer wants to be declared mentally fit to stand trial, according to statements made by his lawyer during a hearing on Monday. The case concerns an...

item.title: Australian Government's Fuel Tax Cut Raises Inflation Worries
item.description: The Australian government's recent decision to temporarily reduce the fuel excise tax has provided some relief to consumers facing rising cost-of-living pressures. The six-month reduction, amounting t...

item.title: PCB fines Naseem Shah Rs20m for breaching central contract after social media post about Punjab CM
item.description: Lahore: Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has imposed a fine of Rs20 million on fast bowler Naseem Shah for violating multiple clauses of his central contract. The disciplinary action followed a social med...

item.title: Supporting First-in-Family Students to Successfully Graduate from University
item.description: In Australia, roughly 30% of new undergraduates are the first in their families to attend university, meaning their parents do not hold tertiary qualifications. These students often face unfamiliar ch...

item.title: Police seize P9.5 million worth of shabu in Lucena and Zamboanga anti-drug operations
item.description: Philippine authorities successfully seized a total of P9.5 million worth of shabu from operations in Lucena City and Zamboanga City over the weekend. In Lucena, police conducted a sting in Barangay Ib...

item.title: Nigeria Crude Oil Exports to US Drop Sharply in January 2026 Amid Global Energy Challenges
item.description: For January 2026, Nigeria see big drop for crude oil wey e dey export go United States. U.S. Census Bureau and Bureau of Economic Analysis show say imports from Nigeria fall almost 47 per cent from De...

item.title: Partner of Scottish gang leader Steven Lyons detained in Dubai amid international arrests
item.description: Amanda Lyons, the partner of notorious Scottish gang leader Steven Lyons, was arrested in Dubai in connection with alleged offences committed in Spain. Her detention followed an Interpol red alert iss...

item.title: Guidelines for Applying to Startup Battlefield 2026
item.description: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield 2026 seeks the most promising early-stage startups rather than the most polished. The program highlights companies with unique, category-defining ideas that can meanin...

item.title: President Samia acknowledges gains in public financial management and calls for further reforms
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has expressed satisfaction with improvements in Tanzania’s public financial management, following the latest report from the Controller and Auditor General (CAG). The rep...

item.title: Family mourns Chloe Watson Dransfield following fatal stabbing in Leeds
item.description: The family of 16-year-old Chloe Watson Dransfield, who tragically died after being stabbed in Leeds, have publicly paid tribute to her. Chloe, from Gomersal, West Yorkshire, was found unconscious on K...

item.title: South Africa’s NHI Faces Legal Delays as Government Seeks Dialogue with Doctors and Business
item.description: Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi has opened discussions with the South African Medical Association (SAMA), one of several organisations challenging the National Health Insurance (NHI) Act in court. Th...

item.title: Israel Approves Death Penalty Law for Palestinian Terror Suspects
item.description: Israel's parliament, on Monday, gave the green light to a controversial bill allowing the execution of Palestinians convicted of terror-related offenses resulting in fatalities. The move, pushed by Na...
